metadata.description: A-Level Physics topics with prerequisite ordering, colored by component
sources: Cambridge International AS & A Level Physics 9702 Syllabus

nodes.label: SI Units & Base Quantities
nodes.description: SI base quantities and their units, using SI base units to check homogeneity of physical equations

nodes.label: Scalars & Vectors
nodes.description: Distinguishing scalar and vector quantities, resolving and adding vectors by calculation and graphical methods

nodes.label: Errors & Uncertainties
nodes.description: Systematic and random errors, precision and accuracy, combining uncertainties in calculated results

nodes.label: Displacement, Velocity & Acceleration
nodes.description: Defining displacement, velocity and acceleration; distinguishing instantaneous and average values

nodes.label: Motion Graphs
nodes.description: Displacement-time and velocity-time graphs, determining velocity and acceleration from gradients, displacement from area under v-t graph

nodes.label: Equations of Uniformly Accelerated Motion
nodes.description: Deriving and applying the four kinematic equations (suvat) for uniformly accelerated motion in a straight line

nodes.label: Free Fall & Projectile Motion
nodes.description: Acceleration of free fall, motion under gravity with and without air resistance, projectile motion as independent horizontal and vertical components

nodes.label: Newton's Laws of Motion
nodes.description: Newton's first, second and third laws; relating net force to mass and acceleration (F = ma), identifying action-reaction pairs

nodes.label: Mass & Weight
nodes.description: Distinguishing mass and weight, gravitational field strength g, weight = mg

nodes.label: Momentum & Impulse
nodes.description: Linear momentum p = mv, impulse as force x time and change in momentum, Newton's second law in terms of rate of change of momentum

nodes.label: Conservation of Momentum
nodes.description: Principle of conservation of momentum, elastic and inelastic collisions, applying conservation in one and two dimensions

nodes.label: Types of Force
nodes.description: Gravitational, electrostatic, magnetic, contact, frictional and viscous forces; drag and terminal velocity

nodes.label: Turning Effects & Equilibrium
nodes.description: Moment of a force, torque of a couple, conditions for equilibrium (zero resultant force and zero resultant torque), centre of gravity

nodes.label: Density & Pressure
nodes.description: Density and pressure definitions, pressure in fluids (p = rho g h), upthrust and Archimedes' principle

nodes.label: Work Done by a Force
nodes.description: Work done W = Fs cos(theta), work done as energy transferred, calculating work from force-displacement graphs

nodes.label: Kinetic & Potential Energy
nodes.description: Kinetic energy (1/2 mv^2), gravitational potential energy (mgh), elastic potential energy, deriving kinetic energy from work-energy theorem

nodes.label: Conservation of Energy
nodes.description: Principle of conservation of energy, energy transformations, dissipation of energy in real systems

nodes.label: Power & Efficiency
nodes.description: Power as rate of work done (P = Fv), efficiency as ratio of useful output to total input energy or power

nodes.label: Stress, Strain & Young Modulus
nodes.description: Tensile/compressive stress and strain, Young modulus as gradient of stress-strain graph, experimental determination

nodes.label: Elastic & Plastic Deformation
nodes.description: Hooke's law, spring constant, elastic limit, elastic and plastic behaviour, strain energy from area under force-extension graph

nodes.label: Progressive Waves
nodes.description: Transverse and longitudinal waves, displacement-distance and displacement-time graphs, wave equation v = f lambda

nodes.label: Wave Intensity & Energy
nodes.description: Intensity as power per unit area, intensity proportional to amplitude squared, inverse-square law for point sources

nodes.label: Doppler Effect for Sound
nodes.description: Qualitative and quantitative treatment of the Doppler effect for sound waves, applications

nodes.label: Electromagnetic Spectrum
nodes.description: Properties of electromagnetic waves, orders of magnitude of wavelengths of the electromagnetic spectrum, polarisation of transverse waves

nodes.label: Reflection & Refraction
nodes.description: Laws of reflection and refraction, refractive index, Snell's law, total internal reflection and critical angle

nodes.label: Principle of Superposition
nodes.description: Superposition of waves, constructive and destructive interference, path difference and phase difference

nodes.label: Two-Source Interference
nodes.description: Young's double-slit experiment, conditions for observable interference, fringe spacing equation lambda = ax/D

nodes.label: Diffraction & Diffraction Gratings
nodes.description: Single-slit diffraction, diffraction grating equation d sin(theta) = n lambda, resolving power, using gratings to measure wavelength

nodes.label: Stationary Waves
nodes.description: Formation of stationary waves by superposition, nodes and antinodes, harmonics on strings and in air columns

nodes.label: Charge & Current
nodes.description: Electric charge, elementary charge, current as rate of flow of charge, I = nAvq (drift velocity equation)

nodes.label: Potential Difference & EMF
nodes.description: Potential difference as energy per unit charge, electromotive force, distinguishing p.d. and e.m.f.

nodes.label: Resistance & Resistivity
nodes.description: Ohm's law, resistance R = V/I, resistivity rho = RA/L, I-V characteristics of ohmic and non-ohmic components

nodes.label: Electrical Power & Energy
nodes.description: Power dissipated P = IV = I^2 R = V^2/R, energy transferred in electrical circuits, kilowatt-hour

nodes.label: Kirchhoff's Laws
nodes.description: Kirchhoff's first law (conservation of charge) and second law (conservation of energy) applied to circuits

nodes.label: Series & Parallel Circuits
nodes.description: Combined resistance in series and parallel, current and voltage rules for series and parallel combinations

nodes.label: Potential Dividers
nodes.description: Potential divider circuits, using thermistors and LDRs in potential dividers for sensing applications

nodes.label: EMF & Internal Resistance
nodes.description: Internal resistance of a source, terminal p.d. vs e.m.f., determining e.m.f. and internal resistance experimentally

nodes.label: Atomic Structure & Isotopes
nodes.description: Proton number, nucleon number, isotopes, nuclear notation, relative masses and charges of subatomic particles

nodes.label: Nuclear Processes
nodes.description: Nuclear reactions including fission and fusion, balancing nuclear equations, mass-energy equivalence at introductory level

nodes.label: Radioactive Decay & Half-Life
nodes.description: Alpha, beta and gamma radiation; properties and detection, random nature of decay, half-life, activity

nodes.label: Quarks, Leptons & Particle Classification
nodes.description: The standard model overview, quarks (up, down, strange), leptons, hadrons (baryons and mesons), antiparticles

nodes.label: Particle Interactions & Conservation Laws
nodes.description: Conservation of charge, baryon number, lepton number and strangeness in particle interactions, beta decay in terms of quarks

nodes.label: Angular Displacement & Velocity
nodes.description: Radian measure, angular displacement, angular velocity (omega = 2 pi / T), relating linear and angular velocity (v = r omega)

nodes.label: Centripetal Acceleration & Force
nodes.description: Centripetal acceleration a = v^2/r = r omega^2, centripetal force, applications to banked tracks, vertical circles

nodes.label: Gravitational Field Strength
nodes.description: Gravitational field as a field of force, field lines, gravitational field strength g = F/m, uniform and radial fields

nodes.label: Newton's Law of Gravitation
nodes.description: Newton's law of gravitation F = GMm/r^2, gravitational constant G, combining with field strength for g = GM/r^2

nodes.label: Gravitational Potential & Energy
nodes.description: Gravitational potential phi = -GM/r, gravitational potential energy, escape velocity, potential-distance graphs

nodes.label: Orbits & Geostationary Satellites
nodes.description: Orbital motion, equating gravitational force to centripetal force, geostationary orbits, Kepler's third law derivation

nodes.label: Thermal Equilibrium & Temperature Scales
nodes.description: Thermal equilibrium, thermometric properties, Celsius and thermodynamic (kelvin) temperature scales

nodes.label: Specific Heat Capacity
nodes.description: Internal energy as sum of kinetic and potential energies, specific heat capacity, Q = mc delta T, experimental methods

nodes.label: Specific Latent Heat
nodes.description: Phase changes, specific latent heat of fusion and vaporisation, Q = mL, heating curves

nodes.label: Gas Laws
nodes.description: Boyle's law, Charles's law, pressure law, experimental verification, absolute zero

nodes.label: Ideal Gas Equation
nodes.description: Equation of state pV = nRT = NkT, mole and Avogadro constant, Boltzmann constant, conditions for ideal gas behaviour

nodes.label: Kinetic Theory of Gases
nodes.description: Assumptions of kinetic theory, deriving pV = (1/3)Nm<c^2>, mean translational kinetic energy (3/2)kT, root-mean-square speed

nodes.label: First Law of Thermodynamics
nodes.description: Internal energy, first law delta U = q + W, applying to isothermal, adiabatic, isobaric and isovolumetric processes

nodes.label: Simple Harmonic Motion
nodes.description: Defining SHM (a = -omega^2 x), displacement, velocity and acceleration equations, graphical representations, period and frequency

nodes.label: Energy in SHM
nodes.description: Interchange between kinetic and potential energy during SHM, total energy of an oscillator, energy-displacement graphs

nodes.label: Damping & Resonance
nodes.description: Free and forced oscillations, light/heavy/critical damping, resonance and natural frequency, amplitude-frequency graphs, applications

nodes.label: Communication Channels
nodes.description: Analogue and digital signals, advantages of digital transmission, channels: wire pairs, coaxial cables, optic fibres, radio waves

nodes.label: Modulation & Signal Transmission
nodes.description: Amplitude modulation (AM) and frequency modulation (FM), bandwidth, signal attenuation in dB, regeneration and amplification

nodes.label: Electric Field Concept
nodes.description: Electric field as a field of force, field lines for point charges and parallel plates, electric field strength E = F/q

nodes.label: Coulomb's Law
nodes.description: Coulomb's law F = kQ1Q2/r^2, permittivity of free space, applying to point charges

nodes.label: Uniform Electric Fields
nodes.description: Electric field between parallel plates E = V/d, motion of charged particles in uniform fields, deflection

nodes.label: Electric Potential & Energy
nodes.description: Electric potential V = kQ/r, electric potential energy, potential-distance graphs, equipotential surfaces, relationship between field and potential

nodes.label: Capacitance & Capacitors
nodes.description: Capacitance C = Q/V, parallel plate capacitor, capacitors in series and parallel, factors affecting capacitance

nodes.label: Energy Stored in Capacitors
nodes.description: Energy stored W = (1/2)QV = (1/2)CV^2 = Q^2/(2C), area under Q-V graph, uses of capacitors as energy stores

nodes.label: Capacitor Charging & Discharging
nodes.description: Exponential charge and discharge curves, time constant RC, equations Q = Q0 e^(-t/RC), determining time constant from graphs

nodes.label: Electronics: Sensing & Switching
nodes.description: Operational amplifiers, comparators, inverting and non-inverting amplifiers, use of op-amps in sensing circuits with thermistors and LDRs

nodes.label: Electronics: Output Devices & Logic
nodes.description: Relays, output transducers (LED, buzzer, motor), feedback in op-amp circuits, gain and bandwidth

nodes.label: Magnetic Flux Density
nodes.description: Magnetic field concept, magnetic flux density B, representing fields with field lines, fields due to current-carrying conductors and solenoids

nodes.label: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor
nodes.description: Force F = BIL sin(theta), Fleming's left-hand rule, defining the tesla, force between parallel current-carrying conductors

nodes.label: Force on a Moving Charge
nodes.description: Force F = BQv sin(theta), circular motion of charged particles in magnetic fields, velocity selector, Hall effect and Hall voltage

nodes.label: Magnetic Flux & Flux Linkage
nodes.description: Magnetic flux phi = BA cos(theta), flux linkage N phi, units of weber and weber-turn

nodes.label: Faraday's & Lenz's Laws
nodes.description: Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction (EMF = -d(N phi)/dt), Lenz's law, applications to generators, eddy currents

nodes.label: Alternating Current Characteristics
nodes.description: Sinusoidal AC, peak and RMS values of current and voltage, power in AC circuits, I_rms = I_0 / sqrt(2)

nodes.label: Transformers
nodes.description: Structure and operation of transformers, turns ratio equation, efficiency of real transformers, power transmission at high voltage

nodes.label: Rectification & Smoothing
nodes.description: Half-wave and full-wave rectification using diodes, smoothing with capacitors, effect of load resistance and capacitance on ripple

nodes.label: Photon Energy & the Photon Model
nodes.description: Photon model of electromagnetic radiation, E = hf = hc/lambda, electronvolt as unit of energy

nodes.label: Photoelectric Effect
nodes.description: Observations of the photoelectric effect, Einstein's photoelectric equation hf = phi + KE_max, threshold frequency, work function

nodes.label: Energy Levels & Line Spectra
nodes.description: Discrete energy levels in atoms, emission and absorption spectra, photon emission/absorption hf = E1 - E2

nodes.label: Wave-Particle Duality
nodes.description: de Broglie wavelength lambda = h/p, electron diffraction as evidence, wave-particle duality of light and matter

nodes.label: Mass-Energy Equivalence
nodes.description: Einstein's mass-energy relation E = mc^2, atomic mass unit (u), mass defect, calculating energy released in nuclear reactions

nodes.label: Nuclear Binding Energy
nodes.description: Binding energy and binding energy per nucleon, binding energy curve, stability of nuclei, explaining fission and fusion energetically

nodes.label: Nuclear Fission
nodes.description: Induced fission, chain reactions, critical mass, controlled fission in nuclear reactors, fuel rods, moderator, control rods

nodes.label: Nuclear Fusion
nodes.description: Conditions for fusion, plasma confinement, fusion in stars, energy output per nucleon compared to fission, challenges of controlled fusion

nodes.label: Radioactive Decay Law
nodes.description: Exponential decay N = N0 e^(-lambda t), decay constant lambda, activity A = lambda N, half-life t_1/2 = ln2/lambda

nodes.label: Medical Imaging: X-rays
nodes.description: Production of X-rays, X-ray attenuation I = I0 e^(-mu x), contrast media, CT scanning principles and image construction

nodes.label: Medical Imaging: Ultrasound
nodes.description: Generation and detection of ultrasound, acoustic impedance Z = rho c, reflection at boundaries, A-scan and B-scan imaging

nodes.label: Medical Imaging: MRI & PET
nodes.description: Principles of magnetic resonance imaging (nuclear spin, precession, relaxation), positron emission tomography, use of tracers, comparison of imaging techniques
